# Emulsification

An emulsion is a stable mixture of two liquids that normally separate, such as oil
and water. Emulsification disperses one liquid as tiny droplets throughout the other
and keeps them suspended. Mayonnaise, vinaigrette, butter and milk are everyday
emulsions that cooks rely on for smooth texture and even flavour.

## How emulsifiers work

Droplets want to merge back together, so an emulsifier is needed to keep them apart.
Emulsifiers such as egg yolk lecithin or mustard have one end that binds water and
another that binds oil; they coat each droplet and stop it from coalescing. Whisking
vigorously creates the small droplets, and the emulsifier then holds the sauce
together instead of letting it split.
